title: FIX-457: Fix Staging Branch Requirement for Library Repositories
description: This tour walks through the changes introduced by FIX-457, which addresses a two-part oversight in the helix-cli build process. First, the build system was unconditionally requiring a staging branch for all repository types, but library repositories have no staging concept — this was causing cascading errors whenever a library repo was processed. Second, even for standard code repositories, the system was forcing users to manually create a staging branch rather than automatically deriving it from main. The tour covers the ticket context and resolution notes in logical order, explaining the motivation and impact of each change.

steps.description: This is the original ticket context file for FIX-457. It establishes the run branch (helix/fix/FIX-457-new-bug-with-library-system-architecture) and links back to parent ticket FIX-441 (Library System Architecture Checkup). Reading this file first gives you the full problem scope before examining any code changes: the build process had an unconditional staging-branch requirement that was wrong for two distinct reasons.
steps.title: Overview: Ticket Origin and Scope

steps.description: Lines 18-20 describe the first root cause: library repositories do not follow a staging workflow. The build system was demanding a staging branch unconditionally, producing a cascade of errors (evidenced by the attached screenshot) whenever a library repo was processed. The fix must introduce a repository-type guard so that staging-branch logic is skipped entirely for library repos.
steps.title: Root Cause 1: Libraries Have No Staging Branch

steps.description: Lines 22-23 describe the second root cause: even for standard code repositories, requiring the user to manually create a staging branch is unnecessary friction. The corrected behaviour should automatically derive a staging branch off main, eliminating a manual step that was error-prone and not documented anywhere in the onboarding flow.
steps.title: Root Cause 2: Manual Staging Branch Creation Is Unnecessary

steps.description: This is the second-run ticket context file. It carries the same ticket and branch identifiers but now includes a 'Referenced Tickets' section that materialises FIX-441 artifacts under .helix-refs/FIX-441/. This is important because FIX-441 defined the library system architecture that this fix builds upon — the agent needed those prior artifacts to understand the repository-type model before implementing the guard logic.
steps.title: Second Run: Referenced Ticket Artifacts Materialised

steps.description: Lines 27-34 show that FIX-441 (status: DEPLOYED, 15 artifacts) was fully materialised at .helix-refs/FIX-441/ before the agent began work. This ensures the fix is grounded in the authoritative library architecture definition from the prior ticket rather than making assumptions, maintaining consistency across the two related changes.
steps.title: FIX-441 Artifacts: Architecture Foundation

steps.description: The Discussion entry (line 39) records the agent's completion note: changes were applied to 1 repository but full automated verification could not be completed. This means a manual review pass is required to confirm that (a) library repos no longer trigger staging-branch errors and (b) standard code repos have their staging branch auto-created from main. Use this tour as the starting point for that manual review.
steps.title: Completion Note: Manual Verification Required

steps.description: This is the intraview walkthrough JSON generated by the agent as part of its run artefacts. It documents the agent's own step-by-step reasoning about the changes, serving as a secondary reference alongside this CodeTour. Cross-referencing both tours confirms the agent's intent matches the actual diff and that no steps were skipped or misunderstood during the fix.
steps.title: Summary: Agent Intraview Walkthrough Cross-Reference
